Title: Install Samba (SMB) on Linux
Description: Install and configure Samba to share folders from Linux to Windows machines

Sections.Content:
## Install Samba on Ubuntu / Debian
Samba allows Linux to share files with Windows machines using the SMB protocol.
### Step 1: Install Samba
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y samba samba-common
### Step 2: Create the directory to share

sudo mkdir -p /srv/samba/$variable:ShareName$
sudo chmod 777 /srv/samba/$variable:ShareName$
### Step 3: Backup and edit the Samba configuration file
sudo cp /etc/samba/smb.conf /etc/samba/smb.conf.bak
sudo nano /etc/samba/smb.conf

### Add the following block at the end of smb.conf:
Replace the values with your settings.

```
[$variable:ShareName$]
   comment = Samba Share
   path = /srv/samba/$variable:ShareName$
   read only = no
   browsable = yes
   guest ok = yes
   valid users = $username$
```

### Step 4: Add a Samba user
The Samba user must also exist as a Linux user.
sudo smbpasswd -a $variable:SambaUsername$
sudo smbpasswd -e $variable:SambaUsername$
### Step 5: Test the Samba configuration
testparm
### Step 6: Restart Samba services

sudo systemctl restart smbd nmbd
sudo systemctl enable smbd nmbd
### Step 7: Allow Samba through the firewall
sudo ufw allow samba
### Check Samba status and list shares

sudo systemctl status smbd
smbclient -L localhost -N

### Connect from Windows
Open File Explorer and type in the address bar:
\\\\SERVER_IP_OR_NAME\\$variable:ShareName$
